_comment_1: Fleet auto-update stagger. Spreads sports-bar-autoupdate.timer fire times across a 2-hour UTC window so the 6-box fleet doesn't hammer Claude API + GitHub + npm registry at the same minute. install-auto-update-timer.sh reads this file (when present and parseable) and overrides the DB scheduleCron with the slot below — UTC times so the schedule survives timezone differences across the fleet (3 boxes are on UTC, 3 on America/Chicago).
_comment_2: Keys are git branch slugs (the 'location/X' branch suffix). Add a new location? Append a slot here, pick a UTC time not within 10 min of any other slot, commit + push. Every location picks it up on the next auto-update finalize step, which re-runs install-auto-update-timer.sh if this file's mtime changed since last run.
_comment_3: 20-minute spacing is conservative — Claude API tier-4 handles 6 simultaneous Opus checkpoint calls fine, but spreading them out keeps GitHub fetch bursts uncongested + makes troubleshooting easier (one location's log file per chronological window).
_comment_4: Local-time equivalents (Central is UTC-5 CDT / UTC-6 CST): holmgren-way 21:00/20:00 CDT, leg-lamp 21:20, lucky-s 21:40, graystone 22:00/21:00 CDT, greenville 22:20, appleton 22:40. All run the evening BEFORE the UTC date stamp on the run log.
